posts.cooked:
BEAT THE '89 ANGELUS - (2/1/2023)

We didn’t know the order of the pairings since the wines were randomised but the best was saved for the last - the '90 Las Cases and '88 LMHB were served in the same flight and were the unanimous wines of the lunch.

  • 2005 Domaine Jamet Côte-Rôtie - France, Rhône, Northern Rhône, Côte-Rôtie
    Brought this as an extra for a friend, was interested to see if a top northern Rhone would stand up to the greats of Bordeaux. I opened this slightly earlier expecting that it needed time, the nose on opening was quite seductive with perfumed, meaty blue fruit. Unfortunately it became muted on both on the nose and palate during lunch without much structure. Am putting this down as corked even though there wasn’t any perceptible sign of TCA. NR (flawed)
  • 1989 Château Angélus - France, Bordeaux, Libournais, St. Émilion Grand Cru
    Quite rich and dark-fruited, unfortunately a little monolithic compared to the '90 Las Cases and '88 LMHB. Still rather tight even with a couple of hours in the glass and wasn’t fully expressive today. Was expecting a more profound wine. (91 pts.)
  • 1993 Château Lafleur - France, Bordeaux, Libournais, Pomerol
    Corked but still drinkable. Could tell this was superb for a '93, very ripe and sweet red fruit. NR (flawed)
  • 2002 Château Angélus - France, Bordeaux, Libournais, St. Émilion Grand Cru
    Had strong notes of hawthorn and tea leaves very similar to '98 Groffier Amoureuses a couple of months ago. Not sure what caused two completely different wines to show so similarly but in both instances this note was quite distracting. In any case this felt over-extracted and the structure of the wine was lacking to support the fruit. (89 pts.)
  • 1990 Château Léoville Las Cases - France, Bordeaux, Médoc, St. Julien
    A stunning wine. Guessed this as 1990 straight off the bat but someone gazumped me to Las Cases! Consistent with the last couple of times I had this, such a sensual and silky wine with perfectly balanced dark red fruit. Wonderful finish. Tied 3-3 for wine of the lunch with the LMHB. (95 pts.)
  • 1988 Château La Mission Haut-Brion - France, Bordeaux, Graves, Pessac-Léognan
    My contribution, was excited to try this after reading positive notes about this vintage in a vertical of LMHB and it didn’t disappoint. The nose was rather muted at first but with air the classic left-bank notes of tobacco and pencil shavings began to show. Very rugged wine with a little rusticity. In comparison to the Las Cases this was definitely a brute, but still showing remarkable restraint. An understated wine that spoke with such clarity, this was clearly a wine from a bygone era and made in a style that I adore. Impressively the taster below nailed it as a HB or LMHB but in comparison to the HBs last week this was definitely more masculine and less aristocratic. Tied 3-3 for wine of the lunch with the Las Cases. (95 pts.)
  • 2011 Dönnhoff Niederhäuser Hermannshöhle Riesling Auslese Goldkapsel - Germany, Nahe
    From half. More exotic apricot and pineapple fruit, less acid-driven than the Willi Schaefer from last week. Very pleasurable. (92 pts.)

Photo 2-1-23, 8 28 49 PM1536×1024 454 KB

NEW YEAR’S DAY DINNER AT EAST BISTRO - (1/1/2023)

  • NV Lancelot-Pienne Champagne Grand Cru Cuvée de la Table Ronde - France, Champagne, Champagne Grand Cru
    Not the most obvious BdB, quite heavy with dominant red apple fruit. Thought this was a little overripe but became more balanced with air. Think out of the three cuvees that I’ve tried the Tradition Brut was my favourite. (90 pts.)
  • 2021 Domaine de la Louvetrie (Landron & Fils) Muscadet de Sèvre-et-Maine Amphibolite - France, Loire Valley, Pays Nantais, Muscadet de Sèvre-et-Maine
    An almost clear colour. Absolutely bone-dry, very linear with tangy citrus fruits. No signs of underripeness or thinness, clean saline finish. Great value and would make a great foil for grilled seafood. (90 pts.)
  • 2019 Peter Lauer Kupp Riesling Faß 18 Großes Gewächs - Germany, Mosel Saar Ruwer
    From screwcap unlike the last bottle which was under cork. Similar impressions to my previous note, still in an awkward phase or maybe GG isn’t my thing - the tiny bit of RS is always very jarring to me. (89 pts.)
  • 2020 Sadie Family Skerpioen - South Africa, Coastal Region, Swartland
    Blend of Palomino and Chenin Blanc. Should have read the previous notes that mentioned this needs time. Similar to what they mentioned, this was very tight and bland to begin with, but after two hours this pulled ahead of the rest of the whites and showed its class. Lovely expression of ripe yellow fruit with proper weight and tension that gave a creamy texture to the wine. Reminded me of well-made Burgundies (with an obviously different profile but hopefully you get my drift). (92 pts.)
  • 2020 Clos des plantes Vin de France Pelo - France, Vin de France
    Rather strange wine, it had 20% Chenin mixed in and you can definitely feel it - seemed like white wine trapped in a red wine’s body. Usual natty nose of wild strawberries. I do like acid but this was way too tart for me. (87 pts.)
  • 2017 Domaine Jean-Marc / Thomas Bouley Bourgogne Hautes-Côtes de Beaune Rouge - France, Burgundy, Bourgogne Hautes-Côtes de Beaune
    First time I am disappointed with a Bouley. Rather thin and acidic, this was definitely underripe. Marginally better with air but this was so austere unlike the usual giving 2017s. Seemed like it was from a cold vintage in the 90s (and not in a good way). (88 pts.)
  • 2010 Château Sénéjac - France, Bordeaux, Médoc, Haut-Médoc
    Rather modern leaning, not my style but others liked it a lot. A little bretty on the nose which was distracting. Dark and rich, plummy fruit with chocolate notes. (89 pts.)
    Photo 2-1-23, 6 45 31 PM1068×754 250 KB

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked: Think you must have had an off bottle of the 2017 Bouley, not at all how that wine normally shows. Thomas is pretty much the last person in the Côte de Beaune who I’d expect to hear accused of picking underripe, given his philosophy!

posts.name: Jeff Leve
posts.cooked: You find 89 Angelus tight? I find the wine to be very expressive. I don’t think it’s ever been tight. Recent tastings have showed 89 to be slightly past its prime these days. But that’s just me. I’m with on the 90 LLC! Great wine …

posts.name: Ilkka |_ |_| mme
posts.cooked:
melvinyeo:

or maybe GG isn’t my thing

Or maybe Mosel GG isn’t your thing! I certainly enjoy this level more from say Pfalz or Rheinhessen than Mosel on average.

posts.name: Mark Golodetz
posts.cooked: Fun theme. My last note on Angelus ‘89 was in 2011, and I thought then it was close to peaking, but tasty nevertheless.

posts.name: brodie thomson
posts.cooked:
Happy New Year Melvin, the combo of 1988 and LMHB sounds really good.

I always have a soft spot for the 1988 vintage, even if it is less ripe that 1989 and 1990, I have always liked the balance and style.

Cheers brodie

Cheers William for your input - I just checked previous notes on CT and there seems to be a similar trend for this wine at least, unfortunately usually a big fan of Bouley as well thanks to your recommendation!
Unfortunately it wasn’t very expressive and seemed reserved, perhaps an off-bottle. Glad yours and @Mark_Golodetz’s experiences were better. And yes 90 LLC has been consistently great!
Perhaps Ilkka! I do have a few Wittmanns that I am waiting to come around any Pfalz producers that you recommend as that’s an area I am less familiar with?
Happy new year too Brodie! And definitely love vintages like 1988 too, think many feel it is an off-vintage but I think it is an overlooked rather than off-vintage.

posts.name: Ilkka |_ |_| mme
posts.cooked:
melvinyeo:

Perhaps Ilkka! I do have a few Wittmanns that I am waiting to come around any Pfalz producers that you recommend as that’s an area I am less familiar with?

Well Dr. Bürklin-Wolf I am sure you are familiar with but others definitely worth checking out are Koehler-Ruprecht, Rings and Dr. Von Bassermann Jordan. Also in Rheinhessen Schätzel is one that is less well known than Keller and Wittmann.
Thanks Ilkka, will keep a lookout for these as our palates seem to align! Don’t think those are available in Singapore but will look out for those overseas
